# Generated by ai-coding-rules v0.1.0
# Source: ai-coding-rules.config.json
# Do not edit manually — run 'ai-coding-rules generate' to regenerate
# https://github.com/self-made4/ai-coding-rules


# ai-coding-rules

CLI tool that generates AI coding rules for every agent from a single config

## General Guidelines

- Use typescript with strict type checking
- Write tests using vitest

## Code Style

- Use 2 spaces for indentation
- Single quotes
- No semicolons
- Max line length: 100

## Naming Conventions

- Files: kebab-case
- Functions: camelCase
- Constants: UPPER_SNAKE_CASE
- Types: PascalCase

## Code Examples

### Preferred Pattern
```typescript
// Good: Use camelCase for functions
export function getUserData() { ... }
```

### Avoid
```typescript
// Bad: Non-standard naming
export function get_user_data() { ... }
```

## Project Structure

- src/commands — CLI command handlers (init, generate, sync, doctor)
- src/scanners — Project auto-detection scanners
- src/generators — Agent-specific file generators
- src/types — TypeScript types and Zod schemas
- src/utils — Shared utilities (logger, file, prompt)
- tests — Test files mirroring src/ structure

## Guidelines

- Use TypeScript strict mode
- Write JSDoc comments for all public functions
- Keep files under 200 lines
- Use custom error classes for error handling

## Important: Do NOT

- Use 'any' type
- Use synchronous file I/O
- Scan node_modules, .git, or dist directories

## Additional Guidelines

- Follow the strategy pattern for generators
- All generated files must include a watermark header
